properties.remark: SHERIFF REPORTED HAIL AT MILE MARKER 56 ON HIGHWAY 200 LASTED ABOUT 5 MINUTES. HAD TO PULL OVER BECAUSE IT WAS SO HEAVY.
SPOTTERS REPORT A COTTONWOOD TREE FALLEN ACROSS THE ROAD.
SPOTTER REPORTED ESTIMATED WIND GUST OF 60 MPH FROM STORM. SMALLER TREE BRANCHES DOWN WITH THE LARGEST BRANCH ESTIMATED AT 4 INCHES.
1.25 INCH HAIL REPORTED AT LINDBERGH LAKE. STRIPPING SMALL BRANCHES OFF PINES AND COVERING THE GROUND.
SWAN RIVER LODGE REPORTS PERIOD OF INCH SIZE HAIL...ACCOMPANIED WITH PEA AND MARBLE SIZES ALSO.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL REPORTED THAT LASTEST 10 TO 15 MINS IN COTTONWOOD.
MEDIA SHARED PHOTOS OF BACKYARD TRAMPOLINE BLOWN UP AGAINST SIDE OF HOME. DAMAGE TO HOME IS UNKNOWN AT TIME.
NWS EMPLOYEES MEASURED INCH SIZE HAIL AT THE WEATHER FORECAST OFFICE.
NWS EMPLOYEE MEASURED ONE INCH SIZE HAIL AT RESIDENCE NORTH OF MISSOULA IN THE PROSPECT CREEK NEIGHBORHOOD.
POWER OUT IN THE FLORENCE AREA REPORTED BY NWS EMPLOYEE.
POWER LINES DOWN NEAR HIGHWAY 93 AND CARLTON CREEK ROAD BETWEEN LOLO AND FLORENCE.
1 INCH HAIL ESTIMATED BY THE COOP OBSERVER
1 INCH HAIL ESTIMATED BY THE COOP OBSERVER AT GEORGETOWN LAKE
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL ESTIMATED COMING DOWN HARD
SHERRIFFS OFFICE IN DEER LODGE RECEIVED ONE INCH HAIL ESTIMATED.
A FENCING COMPANY IN AVON REPORTED 1 INCH HAIL
STRONG WINDS BLEW THE CHIMENY OFF OF THE SYRINGA LODGE AS WELL AS SOME ON THE TIN OFF FROM THE ROOF.
64 MPH WIND GUST MEASURED BY THE SALMON RAWS STATION.
TWELVE INCH DIAMETER TREE BLOWN DOWN ONTO ROAD.
ESTIMATED WIND GUST UP TO 65 MPH WITH TREES BLOWN DOWN ONTO ROADS. REPORTS OF TREES BLOWN DOWN TOWARDS WHITEFISH.
PUBLIC REPORTED FOUR TREES BLOWN DOWN FROM THUNDERSTORM WINDS AT LAKE MARY RONAN STATE PARK.
TREES BLOWN DOWN ON HIGHWAY 35 NEAR BLUE BAY ALONG FLATHEAD LAKE.

WINDS 60 - 65 MPH CAUSED DOWNED TREES, AS WELL AS SOME FIRE STARTS DUE TO TREES FALLING ON POWER LINES. ELECTRICAL OUTAGES, 2 FIRES IN TOWN DUE TO LIGHTNING AND 3 LIGHTNING CAUSED FIRES BEING MANAGED BY USFS ON TIN CUP ROAD. ALSO HAD A TREE FALL ON A HOUSE.
SEVERAL PINE TREES BENT DOWN/OVER AND BRANCHES ESTIMATED AT 6 INCHES DIAMETER BROKEN OFF OF TREES FROM REPORTED THUNDERSTORMS WINDS AND DOWNBURST.
SPOTTER IN NEZ PERCE REPORTED ONE INCH SIZE HAIL THAT FELL FOR 5 MINS. SEVERAL TREES HAVE BRANCHES DOWN...DIAMETERS AVERAGING ONE TO ONE HALF INCHES.
SPOTTER IN NEZ PERCE REPORTED ONE INCH SIZE HAIL THAT FELL FOR 5 MINS. SEVERAL TREES HAVE BRANCHES DOWN...DIAMETERS AVERAGING ONE TO ONE HALF INCHES.
SPOTTER 2 E DIXON REPORTED HAIL THE SIZE OF QUARTERS WITH SOME LARGER PIECES IN THE MIX.
RELAYED REPORT BY TRAINED SPOTTER...THEIR NEIGHBOR RECEIVED GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL THAT LEFT DENTS IN THEIR CAR AND CAUSED DAMAGED TO THEIR CORN CROP.
SPOTTER REPORTED GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL...WITH SOME LARGER PIECES IN THE MIX IN EAST KAMIAH.
LAW ENFORCEMENT DEPUTY IN KAMIAH REPORTS GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL IN LOLO CREEK AREA.
REPORTS OF GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL IN KAMIAH CITY LIMITS.
REPORTS OF GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL IN WHITEBIRD AND KAMIAH CITY LIMITS AS WELL.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL IN LOLO CREEK AREA. CORRECTION FROM PREVIOUS LSR...TIME OF REPORT WAS 630PM.
HAM RADIO REPORT OF MEASURED ONE INCH HAIL AT OPERATOR RESIDENCE.
RELAYED REPORT FROM NWS EMPLOYEE OF ONE INCH SIZE HAIL NORTH OF FLORENCE.
HEAVY RAIN REPORTED WITH HAIL.
A CAR TRAVELLING OVER LOGAN PASS SUSTAINED DENTS FROM THE HAIL.

A SLOW MOVING THUNDERSTORM DROPPED RAINFALL AMOUNTS BETWEEN 1.59 INCHES UP TO AN ESTIMATED 3.25 INCHES IN ABOUT 1 TO 2 HOURS TIME. TWENTY FIVE HOMES SUSTAINED FLOODING. ONE HOME HAD HALF THEIR YARD WASHED AWAY AS WELL AS FLOODING TO A SHED WHICH WASHED TOOLS AND EQUIPMENT DOWN THE ROAD. ANOTHER HOME HAD THEIR DRIVEWAY WASHED OUT. WATER UP TO 2 FEET DEEP WAS OBSERVED ALONG HOMES IN BOTH CAMAS AND HOT SPRINGS

SQUARE 16 X 16 CINDER BLOCK MOVED COMPLETELY OFF A PLYWOOD SHEET AND PLYWOOD WAS BLOWN AWAY. GUTTERS BLOWN DOWN. METAL LAWN FURNITURE BLOWN OFF OF PORCH. BAILS OF PEAT MOSS ARE BEING BLOWN AROUND. TIME OF DAMAGE IS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR DATA.
MEASURED BY SALMON AWOS
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED SHINGLES BLOWN OFF ROOF IN SALMON AND DOWNED TREES ON HIGHWAY 93 SOUTH BETWEEN SALMON AND CHALLIS
DEER LODGE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED TIN ROOF AND SHINGLE DAMAGE IN ANACONDA. STORM DRAINS WERE ALSO HAVING DIFFICULTY KEEPING UP WITH HEAVY RAINFALL AMOUNTS.
MEASURED BY KRILEY CREEK RAWS STATION
STORM SURVEY OF SKALKAHO PASS 15 HOURS AFTER THE STORM PASSED REVEALED HAIL STONES 1.33 INCH TO 1.5 INCH DIAMETER WITH HAIL ACCUMULATIONS OF OVER 4 INCHES.
A FLASH FLOOD CAME OUT OF EDWARDS GULCH AND BROUGHT DEBRIS, INCLUDING MUD AND ROCK AS WELL AS WATER INTO BUILDINGS ON THE WEST SIDE OF TOWN. THERE IS ALSO SOME BASEMENT FLOODING.
SPOTTER AT THE DEER MOUNTAIN LOOKOUT REPORTED DIME TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL AND HEAVY RAIN.
MEASURED BY THE SALMON RAWS STATION.
MEASURED BY KRILEY CREEK RAWS STATION
RECEIVED PICTURE OF HAIL MEASURED AT ONE AND ONE QUARTER INCH DIAMETER WHICH WAS RELAYED BY BROADCAST MEDIA VIA PUBLIC REPORT
USFS TRAIL CREW REPORT
SEVERE THUNDERSTORM WINDS BLEW DOWN LARGE TREE ONTO CABIN AT THREE RIVERS RESORT AT LOWELL. VERY HEAVY RAIN WAS BLOWING SIDEWAYS.
WIND GUST OF 50 MPH.
ROOF SHINGLES DAMAGED.

NUMEROUS BLOW DOWNS THROUGHOUT CLEARWATER COUNTY. SPOTTER REPORTED 18 AND 20 INCH DIAMETER TREES BROKEN AT BASE AND MID-SECTION RESPECTIVELY. POWER AND PHONE SERVICE KNOCKED OUT. A 26 FOOT AIRSTREAM TRAILER WAS MOVED OFF ITS JACK. FIRE STARTS FROM LIGHTNING WERE ALSO REPORTED FROM THIS STORM.
FOREST SERVICE EMPLOYEE OBSERVED A SMALL TORNADO THAT BRIEFLY TOUCHED THE GROUND WEST OF GREENCREEK.
20 HOMES DESTROYED BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS IN THE HIDDEN VILLAGE AREA WEST OF OROFINOAND IN OROFINO. TREES SNAPPED IN HALF.

WIDESPREAD POWER OUTAGES IN THE OROFINO AREA. TREES REPORTED DOWN NEAR PRISON, HOSPITAL AND HIDDEN VILLAGE AREAS. STORAGE BUILDING BLOWN OUT ON HIGHWAY 12 WEST OF OROFINO. WIND GUSTS ESTIMATED TO BE 60 MPH OR HIGHER NEAR OROFINO AIRPORT WEST OF TOWN.
IDAHO COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED NUMEROUS TREES BLOWN DOWN AND POWER OUT THROUGHOUT IDAHO COUNTY.

SPOTTER REPORTED TREE DAMAGE DUE TO ESTIMATED 60 MPH WIND GUSTS AT RESIDENCE. ALSO SHARED REPORTS FROM LISTENING TO SCANNER OF OTHER DAMAGE AROUND THE OROFINO AREA...INCLUDING TREES ON CARS...ROADS...AND STRUCTURES AROUND THE AREA.
POWER OUT IN WEIPPE DUE TO STRONG WINDS...SPOTTER REPORTED THEIR HOUSE SHAKING DUE TO WINDS...COULD NOT ESTIMATE SPEED.

UPDATE FROM SPOTTER LISTENING TO EMERGENCY SCANNER...SEVERAL TREES ARE DOWN ACROSS HIGHWAYS 11 AND 3 BLOCKING TRAFFIC LANES. ONE AREA TRAILER PARK IS BEING EVACUATED DUE TO TREE DAMAGE ON RESIDENCES. WINDS STILL ELEVATED 40-45 MPH ACROSS THE AREA.
